title: Question 3.2 (Carbonero, Hompe, Moore, and Spirkl)
statement_text: Is the class of digraphs in which every induced directed cycle has length $3$ $\vec{\chi}$-bounded?
context_text: Carbonero, Hompe, Moore, and Spirkl proved that the class of digraphs with no induced directed cycle of odd length at least 5 is not $\vec{\chi}$-bounded, then asked whether requiring every induced directed cycle to have length exactly 3 — a directed analogue of chordal graphs — restores $\vec{\chi}$-boundedness. The present paper is devoted to answering this question negatively.
paper_contribution: Proves (Theorem 1) that the class $\mathcal{C}_3$ of digraphs with no $TT_3$ and no induced directed cycle of length at least 4 has members with arbitrarily large dichromatic number, answering the question negatively and strengthening the construction of [2].
notes: PDF source — $\vec{\chi}$ notation reconstructed from context; exact LaTeX in original may differ.

title: Question 3.3 (Carbonero, Hompe, Moore, and Spirkl)
statement_text: For which integers $k$ is the class of digraphs whose induced directed cycles all have length exactly $k$ $\vec{\chi}$-bounded?
context_text: The authors note that Theorem 1 settles the case $k=3$ in the negative, and they leave the problem open for all $k \geq 4$. This question was posed by Carbonero, Hompe, Moore, and Spirkl as Question 3.3 in [2].
paper_contribution: Resolves the case $k=3$: the class is not $\vec{\chi}$-bounded; the problem remains open for $k \geq 4$.
notes: PDF source — $\vec{\chi}$ notation reconstructed from context.

title: Conjecture 1 (Aboulker, Charbit, and Naserasr)
statement_text: For every oriented tree $T$, the class of digraphs with no induced $T$ is $\vec{\chi}$-bounded.
context_text: Recalled in the Further Works section as a directed analogue of the well-known Gyárfás–Sumner conjecture. It is presented as the broader open problem motivating the study of $\vec{\chi}$-boundedness in hereditary classes of digraphs.
paper_contribution: Paper recalls the conjecture as broader context without making direct progress on it.
notes: Stated with an explicit 'Conjecture' label and citation [1]. Note that Pierre Aboulker is both a current paper author and one of the original conjecture authors (the two roles are distinct publications).

title: Open problem on construction size
statement_text: It would be interesting to know if the size of the $(k+1)$-dichromatic example in $\mathcal{C}_3$ can be reduced below the current bound of $n^{2^{\mathrm{poly}(n)}}$, which is larger than the $2^{\mathrm{poly}(|G_k|)}$ bound achieved by Zykov's construction for triangle-free graphs.
context_text: The authors observe in the Further Works section that their Zykov-style digraph construction yields graphs whose size grows much faster than the analogous triangle-free undirected graphs, and they flag reducing this size as an open problem.
notes: PDF source — the size expression appears as 'n2poly(n)' in the extraction; the exact exponent tower has been reconstructed as $n^{2^{\mathrm{poly}(n)}}$ based on the Lemma 3 bound $n^{4r}$ and the recursive structure, but may differ from the original LaTeX.
